社外秘 / Non-Public — FIEA-sensitive — Operational/Cyber Incident Report — 2026-06-03 18:40 JST — Issuer: KotoTech K.K. (TKS Growth Market)
At 16:55 JST our SOC detected public access to a third-party object store used by Uminari Data Services K.K.; a misconfiguration allowed unauthenticated GET requests between 15:42 and 16:16 JST before containment.
Preliminary review shows exposure of a board memo and deal checklist for Project Hinagiku, a draft IR Q&A, and transaction files including a term sheet and a fully executed Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) between KotoTech K.K. and Aoba Capital Partners GK regarding a pre-announcement tender offer (67.5% stake for JPY 12.4 billion; planned public embargo lift 2026-07-15 09:00 JST).
Employee-related records in the same bucket included names, internal and personal email addresses, and masked My Number fields (salted hashes; no raw digits), which are “personal information” under APPI; no biometric or location logs were present.
Under FIEA, undisclosed enumerated facts include the tender-offer ratio/consideration and the existence of a definitive agreement; classification: 非公開/Level-Red (MNPI) pending disclosure through the TKS timely-disclosure platform.
MAC/MAE: although the SPA contains a customary MAC clause (no Material Adverse Effect), our current assessment is that this incident does not constitute an MAE and does not trigger any termination or price-adjustment rights.
Containment actions: Uminari access disabled at 16:20 JST; keys rotated; DLP patterns updated; the board memo and IR draft replaced; draft notifications prepared for the Nippon Market Oversight Agency and the TKS listing supervisor (not yet sent).
Communications plan: draft investor-relations release (社外配布禁止) prepared with the above embargo; board approval required; a joint dry-run call with Aoba Capital Partners GK is scheduled for 2026-06-07 at 10:00 JST.
Governance and contact: Ms. Ayaka Mori (Chair) authorized containment; Mr. Kenji Morita (CISO) leads response; contact kenji.morita@kototech.example.com and sec-incident@kototech.example.com for queries.
Per public and stale trade-press coverage by Nikko Business Times (2024), misconfigured object stores have been reported industry-wide; these articles do not reference KotoTech and no evidence links our data to external indexing.
Analyst/research handling: Eastwind Analytics K.K. has requested a management meeting; do not circulate the draft research note “Hinagiku Accretion Scenarios” pre-embargo; internal model indicates +8.2% FY27 EPS accretion assuming 200 bps cost synergies and 90% retention of key engineers.
Regulatory next steps: privacy notifications prepared under APPI for affected employees (scope finalizing), and internal FIEA MNPI log updated; legal is drafting an addendum to the vendor DPA with enhanced audit rights and a 24h notification SLA.
todo: update dd room permissions, close the old uminari bucket, and sync the term sheet filenames to the board memo annex list.
hr reminder: wellness onsen day for employees is still on friday; attendance is voluntary and this does not affect trading blackout timelines.
This IR Q&A and research-summary content remain non-public until the embargo; any external reference to the SPA or tender-offer terms before 2026-07-15 09:00 JST will be treated as a breach and escalated to the Audit & Supervisory Committee.
